title: DNS Resolution Failure
description: Applications cannot resolve upstream service hostnames after a resolver misconfiguration, causing cascading connection failures.

solution.reasoning: Post-mortem: A routine /etc/resolv.conf update during a VPN rollout pointed nameservers to an internal resolver that was not yet peered with the production DNS zone. Application startup succeeded, but every outbound call to *.svc.internal failed with 'Temporary failure in name resolution'. Health checks passed on localhost endpoints, masking the issue until cross-service traffic ramped. The root cause is a resolver pointing to an unreachable or non-authoritative nameserver, not application bugs. The fix is to verify resolv.conf, test resolution with dig/nslookup, restore the correct nameserver entries, and confirm application connectivity recovers without restart if the resolver is re-read dynamically. In production, add synthetic DNS checks from every node class. Note: this sandbox placeholder simulates chaos via a flag file — run the final command below to pass verification until the full scenario ships.
commands:
sudo tee /etc/resolv.conf <<EOF
nameserver 10.0.0.2
search svc.internal
EOF
